schema.description: A service for comparing product prices across retailers, tracking historical and predicted price trends, and retrieving discount information.

tools.name: get_pid_pos_details
tools.description: Retrieve the product identifier (PID) and position identifier (POS) for a product by searching its name.

name.description: The product name to search for in the catalog.

pid.description: The unique product identifier used to reference this product across all price comparison operations.

pos.description: The product position identifier used in conjunction with PID to uniquely locate the product variant.

name.description: The matched product name from the catalog.

tools.name: compare_price
tools.description: Fetch and compare current prices for a product across multiple retailers.

pid.description: The unique product identifier used to reference this product across all price comparison operations.

pos.description: The product position identifier used in conjunction with PID to uniquely locate the product variant.

max_retailers.description: The maximum number of retailers to include in the price comparison results. Defaults to 10.

pid.description: The unique product identifier used to reference this product across all price comparison operations.

pos.description: The product position identifier used in conjunction with PID to uniquely locate the product variant.

retailers.description: List of retailer pricing information for the specified product.

store_name.description: The name of the retailer offering the product.

price.description: The current listed price of the product at this retailer.

availability.description: The stock availability status of the product at this retailer.

tools.name: price_trend
tools.description: Fetch predicted future price trend data for a product to anticipate upcoming price changes.

pid.description: The unique product identifier used to reference this product across all price comparison operations.

pos.description: The product position identifier used in conjunction with PID to uniquely locate the product variant.

pid.description: The unique product identifier used to reference this product across all price comparison operations.

pos.description: The product position identifier used in conjunction with PID to uniquely locate the product variant.

price_data.description: List of predicted price data points for future dates.

date.description: The future date for the price prediction.

predicted_price.description: The predicted price of the product on the specified date.

tools.name: get_price_history
tools.description: Retrieve historical price data for a product to analyze past price fluctuations across retailers.

pid.description: The unique product identifier used to reference this product across all price comparison operations.

pos.description: The product position identifier used in conjunction with PID to uniquely locate the product variant.

retailers.description: Optional list of retailer names to filter the price history results. If not provided, returns data for all retailers.

pid.description: The unique product identifier used to reference this product across all price comparison operations.

pos.description: The product position identifier used in conjunction with PID to uniquely locate the product variant.

price_history.description: List of historical price records for the product.

date.description: The date when the price was recorded.

price.description: The recorded price of the product on the specified date.

retailer.description: The name of the retailer where the price was recorded.

tools.name: get_discount_info
tools.description: Retrieve discount and promotional information for a product at a specific retailer within a defined time period.

pid.description: The unique product identifier used to reference this product across all price comparison operations.

pos.description: The product position identifier used in conjunction with PID to uniquely locate the product variant.

retailer.description: The name of the retailer to query for discount information.

time_period.description: The time period to query for discounts. Must be one of: 'current' (active discounts), 'past' (expired discounts), or 'upcoming' (future discounts).

pid.description: The unique product identifier used to reference this product across all price comparison operations.

pos.description: The product position identifier used in conjunction with PID to uniquely locate the product variant.

retailer.description: The name of the retailer for which discount information was retrieved.

discounts.description: List of discount records for the product at the specified retailer.

discount_percentage.description: The percentage discount applied to the product.

start_date.description: The start date of the discount period.

end_date.description: The end date of the discount period.

original_price.description: The original price of the product before the discount.

discounted_price.description: The final price of the product after applying the discount.

scenarios.description: Basic scenario with a single product and minimal price data from two retailers
Multiple products with varied availability, historical prices, and price trends
Comprehensive scenario with multiple products, complete price history, trends, and discount information across various retailers
Edge case with empty product catalog and minimal reference data

test_cases.expected: Return product details with pid, pos, and name
Return price comparison data with max 5 retailers

test_cases.test_name: Get price history without retailer filter
Get price history with retailer filter
Get current discount info for Amazon
Get past discount info for Walmart

save_scenario.details: Saved scenario matches the original loaded scenario perfectly. All state variables (products, retailerPrices, priceHistory, priceTrends, discountInfo, retailerList, availabilityStatuses) are preserved correctly.

errors.error_details: The compare_price tool consistently returns empty retailers list even when retailerPrices data exists in the loaded scenario for the requested products (P001, P002, P003)
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: retailers list with store_name, price, and availability fields. Actual: empty retailers list []
errors.root_cause: The API's compare_price method is not correctly retrieving data from the retailerPrices dictionary. Possible issues: (1) The method may be looking up data using incorrect keys or structure, (2) The method may not be properly accessing the loaded scenario state, (3) There may be a mismatch between how data is stored (retailerPrices) vs how it's retrieved

errors.error_details: The price_trend tool returns empty price_data list for P001 even though the scenario contains priceTrends data with 2 predicted price points for this product
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: price_data list with 2 entries containing date, predicted_price, and trend fields. Actual: empty price_data list []
errors.root_cause: The API's price_trend method is not correctly retrieving data from the priceTrends dictionary. Similar to compare_price, this suggests the method is not properly accessing the loaded scenario state or there's a key/structure mismatch

errors.error_details: The get_price_history tool returns empty price_history list for products P001 and P003 even though the scenario contains priceHistory data for both products
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: price_history list with historical records containing date, price, and retailer fields. Actual: empty price_history list []
errors.root_cause: The API's get_price_history method is not correctly retrieving data from the priceHistory dictionary. The issue persists even when filtering by specific retailers, indicating the core data retrieval mechanism is broken

tool_executions.error: Tool returns empty retailers list despite scenario data containing retailerPrices

test_cases.expected: Should return retailers list with Amazon and Best Buy pricing
Should return retailers list with Newegg and Amazon pricing
Should return retailers list with Target pricing

tool_executions.error: Tool returns empty price_data list despite scenario data containing priceTrends for P001

test_cases.expected: Should return price trend data with 2 predicted price points
Should return empty price_data (no trend data in scenario)
test_cases.note: Correctly returns empty for product without trend data

tool_executions.error: Tool returns empty price_history list despite scenario data containing priceHistory

test_cases.expected: Should return 2 historical price records for Amazon
Should return 2 historical price records filtered for Amazon
Should return 2 historical price records for Target
Should return empty discounts (no discount data in scenario)
test_cases.note: Correctly returns empty when no discount data exists

test_cases.expected: Should return empty discounts (no discount data in scenario)
Should return empty discounts (no discount data in scenario)

save_scenario.note: Saved scenario matches original scenario exactly

summary.critical_issues: Three out of five data retrieval tools (compare_price, price_trend, get_price_history) are failing to retrieve data from the loaded scenario despite the data being present and correctly saved. This indicates a systematic issue with how the API methods access the internal state after load_scenario is called.
summary.recommendation: Review the PriceComparisonAPI class implementation to ensure that: (1) load_scenario properly stores data in instance variables, (2) All retrieval methods access the correct instance variables, (3) Data structure keys match between storage and retrieval operations

errors.error_type: Tool Logic Error - Data Retrieval Failure
errors.error_details: The compare_price tool consistently returns empty 'retailers' list even though the scenario contains retailerPrices data for the requested products (P001 has 3 retailers, P005 has 3 retailers)
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: List of retailer objects with store_name, price, and availability. Actual: Empty list []
errors.root_cause: The API method compare_price() is not correctly accessing or returning the retailerPrices data from the loaded scenario state. Possible issues: (1) Data structure mismatch between scenario format and API internal format, (2) Missing data transformation logic, (3) Incorrect key lookup in the state dictionary

errors.error_type: Tool Logic Error - Data Retrieval Failure
errors.error_details: The price_trend tool consistently returns empty 'price_data' list even though the scenario contains priceTrends data for the requested products (P001 has 3 trend entries, P005 has 2 trend entries)
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: List of price trend objects with date, predicted_price, and trend. Actual: Empty list []
errors.root_cause: The API method price_trend() is not correctly accessing or returning the priceTrends data from the loaded scenario state. Similar to compare_price, this suggests a systematic issue with data retrieval across multiple tools

errors.error_type: Tool Logic Error - Data Retrieval Failure
errors.error_details: The get_price_history tool consistently returns empty 'price_history' list even though the scenario contains priceHistory data for the requested products (P001 has 3 history entries, P002 has 2 history entries)
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: List of historical price objects with date, price, and retailer. Actual: Empty list []
errors.root_cause: The API method get_price_history() is not correctly accessing or returning the priceHistory data from the loaded scenario state. This confirms a pattern of data retrieval failures across all data-fetching tools

errors.error_type: Tool Logic Error - Data Retrieval Failure
errors.error_details: The get_discount_info tool consistently returns empty 'discounts' list even though the scenario contains discountInfo data for the requested products and retailers (P001/Newegg has 1 discount, P005/Amazon has 1 discount)
errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: List of discount objects with discount_percentage, start_date, end_date, original_price, and discounted_price. Actual: Empty list []
errors.root_cause: The API method get_discount_info() is not correctly accessing or returning the discountInfo data from the loaded scenario state. This completes the pattern showing all data retrieval tools are failing

tool_executions.error: Tool returns empty discounts list despite scenario data containing discountInfo

save_scenario.note: Saved scenario matches loaded scenario perfectly - state persistence is working correctly

critical_issues: Systematic data retrieval failure across all tools except get_pid_pos_details
The load_scenario successfully stores data (confirmed by save_scenario returning identical data)
However, the API methods (compare_price, price_trend, get_price_history, get_discount_info) cannot access this stored data
Root cause likely in PriceComparisonAPI class implementation - possible disconnect between how load_scenario stores data and how other methods retrieve it
recommendations: Review the PriceComparisonAPI class implementation (Section 2) to verify data storage mechanism in load_scenario()
Check if API methods are using correct attribute names or dictionary keys to access stored data
Verify that load_scenario() is properly initializing all required internal data structures
Add debug logging to trace data flow from load_scenario through to retrieval methods
Consider if there's a missing initialization step or data transformation between storage and retrieval

Possible issues: (1) Data structure mismatch between scenario format and API internal format, (2) Missing data transformation logic, (3) Incorrect key lookup in the state dictionary

high_tool_logic_errors.error_type: Tool Logic Error - Data Retrieval Failure
high_tool_logic_errors.error_details: The price_trend tool consistently returns empty 'price_data' list even though the scenario contains priceTrends data for the requested products (P001 has 3 trend entries, P005 has 2 trend entries)
high_tool_logic_errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: List of price trend objects with date, predicted_price, and trend. Actual: Empty list []
high_tool_logic_errors.root_cause: The API method price_trend() is not correctly accessing or returning the priceTrends data from the loaded scenario state. Similar to compare_price, this suggests a systematic issue with data retrieval across multiple tools

high_tool_logic_errors.error_type: Tool Logic Error - Data Retrieval Failure
high_tool_logic_errors.error_details: The get_price_history tool consistently returns empty 'price_history' list even though the scenario contains priceHistory data for the requested products (P001 has 3 history entries, P002 has 2 history entries)
high_tool_logic_errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: List of historical price objects with date, price, and retailer. Actual: Empty list []
high_tool_logic_errors.root_cause: The API method get_price_history() is not correctly accessing or returning the priceHistory data from the loaded scenario state. This confirms a pattern of data retrieval failures across all data-fetching tools

high_tool_logic_errors.error_type: Tool Logic Error - Data Retrieval Failure
high_tool_logic_errors.error_details: The get_discount_info tool consistently returns empty 'discounts' list even though the scenario contains discountInfo data for the requested products and retailers (P001/Newegg has 1 discount, P005/Amazon has 1 discount)
high_tool_logic_errors.expected_vs_actual: Expected: List of discount objects with discount_percentage, start_date, end_date, original_price, and discounted_price. Actual: Empty list []
high_tool_logic_errors.root_cause: The API method get_discount_info() is not correctly accessing or returning the discountInfo data from the loaded scenario state. This completes the pattern showing all data retrieval tools are failing
